title: Compound V pre-eval record
description:
Canonical, write-once control-plane record written by the pre-eval scoring engine (A3) to docs/superpowers/pre-eval/<pre_eval_id>.json BEFORE any run dir exists (AC-2). `status: PRE_EVAL_DONE` is a RECORD field, NOT a state.json phase (AC-7/CR2-8) — there is no state.json at prediction time. `decision` only ever OFFERS FASTPATH_ELIGIBLE; the request-level score never auto-routes (Iron-Invariant #4). Enforcement fields are deterministic (taxonomy/localization/override logic), never model magnitude (Iron-Invariant #1). C1's fast-path cross-artifact binding (AC-13) verifies pre_eval_id / decision / taxonomy_digest / localization-digest match across this record, the manifest, and the localization artifact.

DIGEST CONVENTION (CR5-6/CR5-7, single-sourced in compound-v-taxonomy.py, documented in docs/superpowers/architecture/pre-eval-config.md): the record's own `digest` = 'sha256:' + sha256(canonical_json(record MINUS the `digest` field)), where canonical_json = json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False, allow_nan=False). `taxonomy_digest` is a DISTINCT convention: 'sha256:' + sha256(RAW taxonomy-snapshot bytes) — a content address of the immutable snapshot, not a re-serialization.

then.description: A FASTPATH_ELIGIBLE record MUST pin a real, non-null taxonomy snapshot + its content-address (a fast-path is impossible without the sensitive-path + content-pattern protections). taxonomy_ref/taxonomy_digest are required and strictly typed here.

pre_eval_id.description: Write-once join key, minted before localization (CR5-10). Convention: 'YYYY-MM-DDThhmmssZ-<slug>-<nonce>'. The stream key for triage-outcomes and the binding key across every fast-path artifact. Independent of run_id (which does not exist yet).

run_id.description: The run this pre-eval later bound to, or null while unbound (a declined/full-pipeline request may never become a run). Set only via the `bind` triage event once the run dir is created; NEVER present at PRE_EVAL_DONE time.

request_slug.description: Slug derived from the request text (not the not-yet-existing plan). Human-readable; part of pre_eval_id.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the record was written.

status.description: Record-status field (prose-only lifecycle token; AC-7/CR2-8). NOT a state.json phase — no run dir exists at prediction time.

taxonomy_version.description: The `version` of the taxonomy snapshot used. Human-readable; the machine binding is `taxonomy_digest`. NULL in the absent-taxonomy FULL_PIPELINE case (there is no taxonomy to version).

taxonomy_ref.description: Repo-relative, normalized, no-'..' path to the IMMUTABLE taxonomy snapshot the score was computed against (pre-run, under docs/superpowers/pre-eval/<pre_eval_id>.taxonomy-snapshot.yaml). A committed regular file (never an escaping symlink). Copied into the run preserving this digest when a fast-path materializes (CR2-6/CR4-2). NULLABLE for the absent-taxonomy case (see taxonomy_digest); the top-level `if decision==FASTPATH_ELIGIBLE` conditional makes it a required, non-null, non-empty string on any fast-path record.

taxonomy_digest.description: 'sha256:'+sha256(RAW snapshot bytes) of taxonomy_ref. On a FASTPATH_ELIGIBLE record it is required and MUST equal the manifest's taxonomy_digest (AC-13). ABSENT-TAXONOMY CASE (spec §2 missing-data table + Iron-Invariant #5): when the taxonomy file/snapshot is absent, malformed, or unavailable there is no snapshot to reference and no bytes to digest, so the engine STILL writes the record (and appends the `predicted` event) with decision=FULL_PIPELINE and `taxonomy_ref: null` / `taxonomy_digest: null` / `taxonomy_version: null`. Such a record validates precisely because these three fields are nullable when decision != FASTPATH_ELIGIBLE; the `if/then` conditional above forbids that null-taxonomy state from ever being FASTPATH_ELIGIBLE. A3/C1/M1 MUST treat the null-taxonomy record as an unconditional FULL_PIPELINE. (`pattern` applies only when the value is a string, so null passes.)

tiers_signalled.description: Which evidence tiers produced a signal (audit trail + `confidence` evidence). NEVER a gating number.

override_fired.description: The Layer-A hard-override row id (1-6, spec §2) that forced FULL_PIPELINE, or null if none fired. A non-null value REQUIRES decision=FULL_PIPELINE.

decision.description: The triage outcome. FASTPATH_ELIGIBLE only OFFERS a proportionate fast-path (Iron-Invariant #4 — never an auto-route). Any override / unknown axis / missing-or-malformed taxonomy / token-cap overrun ⇒ FULL_PIPELINE (fail-closed).

min_sample_status.description: Whether Tier-2 had >= min_sample_count calibrated FAST-PATH-TAKEN outcomes. `insufficient` (the launch default, no data) means Tier-2 is escalation-only (Iron-Invariant #3 counterfactual guard).

confidence.description: Evidence-only confidence label (signalled tiers + localization confidence). Shown as EVIDENCE, never a gating number (spec §2). Optional.

digest.description: Self-integrity digest of THIS record: 'sha256:'+sha256(canonical_json(record without `digest`)). Optional; when present, downstream verifies it with compound-v-taxonomy.record_digest(record, exclude_field='digest').

band.description: A deterministic band — never a raw magnitude (Iron-Invariant #1). `unknown` forces FULL_PIPELINE via Layer-A override #6.

display.description: Derived 1-10 DISPLAY label (low→1-3, med→4-6, high→7-10); band-midpoint or per-tier max. Computed AFTER the decision — never the gate (spec §2).

localization.description: The bounded read-only localization result. When A1 writes it as the standalone committed artifact docs/superpowers/pre-eval/<pre_eval_id>.localization.json, that file has THIS shape and its own canonical-JSON `digest` (record_digest, exclude_field='digest'); that localization-digest is the value bound across manifest+record+artifact (AC-13).

resolved_paths.description: Repo-relative paths the request resolves to. Fast-path REQUIRES exactly one literal normalized path; resolved_paths[0] MUST equal the manifest's sole write_allowed literal (AC-13).

fan_out.description: How many distinct sites/paths the change touches. Fast-path requires fan_out <= pre_eval.fan_out_threshold (Layer-B).

flags.description: Semantic flags from the shared taxonomy classify() (e.g. sensitive_path, content:shared_token, content:a11y, content:feature_flag). Any override-triggering flag forces FULL_PIPELINE.

confidence.description: Localization confidence. `ambiguous`/`failed` ⇒ Layer-A override #1 ⇒ FULL_PIPELINE (Iron-Invariant #2: no `low` without exact localization).
